name: Yoga fallback path batched relation resolution

description: Baseline for yoga-fallback benchmark suite before batching changes
Attempted request-scoped reverse-ref batching loader; failed TypeScript checks and regressed benchmark
Request-scoped reverse-ref batching improved benchmark materially but failed TypeScript on nullable orderBy
Add request-scoped reverse-ref batching with microtask flush, preserving single-parent exact SQL and batching multi-parent Yoga fallback resolution
Initialize per-request linkedRecordCache in Yoga context to share StructuredText link resolution; benchmark improved but Yoga context typing failed
Seed linkedRecordCache in Yoga request context so StructuredText and nested linked-record resolution can share request-scoped cache
Use the request-scoped structured-text envelope loader for published Yoga fallback too, batching envelope materialization across sibling fields
Also batch structured-text envelope materialization inside block-model resolvers; correct but slower than current best
Share linked-record-loader results through linkedRecordCache so links and StructuredText can reuse records; benchmark regressed sharply
Enable root structured-text prefetch for published list queries too; passed checks but slower than current best
Re-run current best configuration to gauge noise; still materially better than baseline but slower than the best observed 76.446ms
Only prefetch published structured-text envelopes for single-record queries; slight regression versus current best
Added request-scoped asset loader across media and SEO paths; promising benchmark but failed TypeScript due to AssetObject field names and stale import
Add request-scoped asset loader for media, media_gallery, SeoField.image, _site favicon, and _seoMetaTags image lookups; correct but large regression on current fallback benchmark
Route StructuredText inline link resolution through request-scoped linked-record microbatch loaders so links across sibling Yoga fields batch together
Re-run current best after StructuredText linked-record microbatching to confirm improvement; result remains near best and well below baseline but not a new best
Tried reducing reverse-ref loader decode work by matching parent IDs on raw rows before deserialization; passed checks but regressed badly
Tried SQL window-function top-N reverse-ref batching for multi-parent link-only cases; checks passed but benchmark regressed to baseline
Seed single-target linked-record cache entries from StructuredText batched resolutions so later field resolvers can reuse them; passed checks but regressed
Noise check rerun of current best configuration; remained well below baseline but above the best observed 74.598ms
Special-case reverse-ref parent bucketing for single link fields to reduce set/JSON work; passed checks but regressed substantially
Fast-path linked-record microbatch flushes for single-target relations by calling batchFetchRecords directly; checks passed but performance collapsed
Precompute reverse-ref filter compiler maps and localized column lists at schema-build time instead of rebuilding them per resolver call; slight regression versus best
